chapterInitiatives.title: Water Cup
chapterInitiatives.summary: Source-backed local water and impact-mapping effort with public Regen Hub updates and a Water Cup Mapping Hypercert.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current Nigeria chapter record links the public Water Cup update thread and Hypercert. Keep downstream impact claims source-bound until steward review adds verified counts.

chapterInitiatives.title: Tech & Sun Round
chapterInitiatives.summary: Solar-powered public goods infrastructure and builder education effort sourced from Greenpill Nigeria's Localism Fund thread.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public Tech & Sun Round thread documents Greenpill Nigeria's work on solar, internet, campus infrastructure, and hands-on builder education. Keep final activation and usage metrics source-bound until steward review adds completed counts.

chapterInitiatives.title: Take the Greenpill with Greenpill Nigeria
chapterInitiatives.summary: A 10-week public ReFi education program supported by the current Nigeria chapter seed and Regen Hub source.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current Nigeria chapter record links the public 10-week education program thread and treats it as chapter-owned education work.

chapterInitiatives.title: Ethereum Localism and Public-Goods Tooling
chapterInitiatives.summary: GreenSofa public-goods tooling, Ethereum Localism education, and civic-tech bridge work in Taiwan.
chapterInitiatives.description: Public GreenSofa sources document Ethereum Localism events, GreenSofa / FAB DAO context, public-goods tooling work, Hypercert dashboard development, Gitcoin/Giveth participation, and civic-tech bridge-building with Taiwan communities.

chapterInitiatives.title: Regenerative Agriculture, Biochar, and Vermicomposting Hub
chapterInitiatives.summary: Greenpill Kenya work around environmental workshops, regenerative agriculture, tree planting, biochar, and vermicomposting.
chapterInitiatives.description: Public Regen Hub posts describe Greenpill Kenya goals for forestry, soil carbon, composting, biochar, environmental workshops, regenerative agriculture, tree planting, cleanups, vermicomposting, and a demo farm / tree nursery partner. Keep numeric impact claims out until steward-reviewed measurements are available.

chapterInitiatives.title: Environmental Monitoring and Cleanup
chapterInitiatives.summary: Source-backed Phangan cleanup and decentralized environmental monitoring work connected to DeCleanup public tooling.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public Phangan update thread connects beach cleanups, Monthly Earth Day activity, DeCleanup origins, and decentralized environmental monitoring analysis. DeCleanup is supporting tooling context; the chapter initiative remains Phangan-owned cleanup and monitoring work.

chapterInitiatives.title: Ethereum 10Y Cape Town
chapterInitiatives.summary: Local Ethereum 10Y celebration and livestream activation with Greenpill listed as a partner on the public event page.
chapterInitiatives.description: The Luma event page names Greenpill as a partner and notes that Greenpill Cape Town would show the global livestream at the Cape Town celebration venue. Keep this as an event activation until the chapter adds post-event outcomes.

chapterInitiatives.title: 10 Years of Ethereum Phangan
chapterInitiatives.summary: Public local event page connected to Greenpill Koh Phangan's regenerative finance and public-goods activity.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current Koh Phangan chapter record links a public Luma event page and keeps the event as a source-backed activation rather than a standalone public route.

chapterInitiatives.title: Web3 Solutions for Local Impact
chapterInitiatives.summary: GreenPill Toronto Consensus-side gathering on Web3, regeneration, public-goods funding, and local impact.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public Luma page describes a GreenPill Toronto fireside chat bringing together investors, builders, academics, nonprofits, and Indigenous leaders to discuss ReFi, Ethereum Localism, public goods funding, and community projects.

chapterInitiatives.title: Reimagining Our Future Meetups
chapterInitiatives.summary: GreenPill Ottawa meetup series introducing regen philosophy, web3 coordination, and positive-sum local action.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public Luma page frames the meetup as a monthly Green Pill community gathering for people exploring regenerative finance, web3 coordination, and local/global positive impact. It is launch-safe as an event/community initiative, not yet an outcomes report.

chapterInitiatives.title: Greenpill NYC Monthly Meetups
chapterInitiatives.summary: Source-backed public-goods education and meetup activity hosted through the Greenpill NYC Luma calendar.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current NYC chapter record identifies monthly meetups from the public Luma calendar, with the Hub and X account kept as supporting chapter context.

chapterInitiatives.title: Agroforestry Task Force and Living Center
chapterInitiatives.summary: Greenpill Brasil's 2024 agroforestry and living-center work, sourced from the public Greenpill Year in Review.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public 2024 Year in Review names Greenpill Brasil's agroforestry task force and community living-center work as a concrete chapter effort. Keep this initiative qualitative until a chapter-owned follow-up source adds precise completion, attendance, food-sovereignty, or biodiversity metrics.

chapterInitiatives.title: La Ligne Verte
chapterInitiatives.summary: Public Green Mission source for environmental coordination around collection, education, and waste valorization.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current Cote d'Ivoire chapter record points at a public La Ligne Verte update and frames the targets as goals rather than completed metrics.

chapterInitiatives.title: Publications and Learning Series
chapterInitiatives.summary: Uncommons education, translation, publication, and public-learning work sourced from the approved Paragraph preview.
chapterInitiatives.description: The approved Paragraph preview documents Uncommons publications and learning work, including the 2025 Community Annual VOID, MycoFi translation and study activity, the P2P Commons Manifesto translation and book tour, Crypto Flight interview volumes, Summer of Protocols CN, the Chiang Mai Cypherpunk and Thinker Assembly, and Open Good recognition. Present this as an ongoing chapter-owned learning and publications initiative without adding unsupported distribution, attendance, or funding claims.

chapterInitiatives.title: ReFi, DeSci, and Community Activation
chapterInitiatives.summary: GreenPill Germany source-backed activity around ReFi meetups, DeSci collaboration, Hypercert education, and chapter reactivation.
chapterInitiatives.description: The public Regen Hub chapter update lists Hamburg and Munich ReFi activity, a first Hypercert launch, DeSci and ReFi at Berlin Science Week, community activation, steward onboarding, and future education campaigns. Treat metrics as qualitative until a newer steward update adds counts or deliverables.

chapterInitiatives.title: Vibe Coding for Good
chapterInitiatives.summary: Collaborative build night at Reimagine Co presented by Greenpill London Ontario on April 28, 2025.
chapterInitiatives.description: The current London Ontario chapter record uses the public TechAlliance event listing as the launch-safe proof for this local build-night initiative.

seo.title: GreenSci · Greenpill Network
seo.description: GreenSci is a public Greenpill guild surface for regenerative science and open research work.

guilds.name: GreenSci
guilds.summary: Open regenerative science, DeSci-oriented knowledge work, and public research coordination linked to the Greenpill network.
guilds.description: GreenSci is a public guild surface for regenerative science and open research work. The launch Library should treat it as a guild with an external home until source-backed local detail content exists.
guilds.oneliner: Open regenerative science and public research coordination.

outputs.label: GreenSci website
outputs.summary: Public guild home for GreenSci research and DeSci-oriented work.

outputs.label: Open research
outputs.summary: Regenerative science and knowledge commons work that can be linked from the public Library.

mandateParagraphs: GreenSci belongs in the public guild set, not as a project or generic resource card.
The Library should link readers to the public GreenSci site until a source-backed local guild detail page is written and approved.

cadence.summary: Public cadence needs steward confirmation before launch copy promises a standing schedule.

principles.title: Open research
principles.body: Make regenerative science work visible through public, reusable knowledge surfaces.

principles.title: Source-backed publishing
principles.body: Do not create a local guild detail page until there is enough approved source material for one.

media.imageAlt: Greenpill publication artwork used for the public Paragraph archive.

seo.title: Writers Guild · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Writers Guild supports field reports, publishing, translations, and public knowledge work across the network.

guilds.name: Writers Guild
guilds.summary: Publishing, storytelling, research, and public sensemaking across the Greenpill network.
guilds.description: The Writers Guild supports essays, field reports, translations, publishing workflows, and editorial work across chapters and guilds. Public sources show Greenpill's Paragraph publication, the long-running podcast, Garden writing needs, and a network-wide push toward clearer public knowledge infrastructure.
guilds.oneliner: Stories, translations, field reports, and public knowledge work for the network.

outputs.label: Field reports
outputs.summary: Chapter and guild updates that turn local work into public learning.

outputs.label: Translations
outputs.summary: Translation work around Greenpill books and chapter resources, including Brasil's Portuguese work.

outputs.label: Publication surfaces
outputs.summary: Greenpill Paragraph, podcast notes, newsletter copy, and public Hub threads.

outputs.label: Tooling research
outputs.summary: The Carbon Copy collaboration explores how ReFi communities use Web3 tools.

mandateParagraphs: The Writers Guild makes the network legible. It turns local experiments, chapter practice, books, podcasts, and builder work into public stories people can reuse.
The launch website should show stories as proof of activity and learning, not as a generic blog feed.
Draft newsletters and internal Drive notes should stay internal until a steward approves them for public publication.

cadence.summary: Public sources confirm Writers Guild activity and publication surfaces, but exact cadence needs steward confirmation before launch.

principles.title: Source-backed storytelling
principles.body: Every story should link to public proof or a steward-approved publication source.

principles.title: Field-first writing
principles.body: Lead with real chapter, guild, and program updates instead of abstract claims.

principles.title: No private drafts in public content
principles.body: Newsletter drafts, Drive docs, raw notes, and submission data stay out of public Keystatic until approved.

publicMembers.name: Matt (mattycompost)
publicMembers.bio: Publicly referenced in Dev Guild / Octant and Garden-oriented research.

connectLinks.label: GitHub

seo.title: Dev Guild · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Dev Guild builds open-source coordination tools for public goods, impact verification, and regenerative communities.

guilds.name: Dev Guild
guilds.summary: The engineering arm of Greenpill Network, building open-source coordination tools for chapters, funders, and regenerative communities.
guilds.description: The Dev Guild ships public tooling for impact measurement, capital allocation, community governance, reputation, and public network surfaces. Public sources connect the guild to Green Goods, GreenWill, Impact Reef, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Octant funding, Gitcoin rounds, and recurring builder programming.
guilds.oneliner: Building open-source coordination tools for the regenerative network.

outputs.label: Impact verification
outputs.summary: Green Goods and related tools help communities document and verify regenerative work.

outputs.label: Capital allocation
outputs.summary: Grant Ships, Cookie Jar, and related experiments explore public-goods funding mechanisms.

outputs.label: Reputation primitives
outputs.summary: GreenWill explores public-good reputation and pay-it-forward activity.

outputs.label: Public goods staking
outputs.summary: PGSP and Squad Staking explore validator rewards as recurring funding for public goods.

outputs.label: Public writing
outputs.summary: The Dev Guild Paragraph documents builder work and grant-facing updates.

mandateParagraphs: The Dev Guild is the engineering arm of Greenpill Network. It designs and ships open-source software that helps chapters and allied communities coordinate without relying on private intermediaries.
The strongest public proof points are Green Goods, GreenWill, Cookie Jar, Grant Ships, Impact Reef, public GitHub repositories, and the Octant / Gitcoin funding trail.

Where existing tools already do the job, the public direction should frame the guild as an integrator and steward of coordination infrastructure rather than a team rebuilding every primitive from scratch.

cadence.summary: Public sources confirm recurring builder programming, but exact call cadence should be steward-confirmed before launch copy promises a standing schedule.
cadence.format: Builder spaces and public async work

principles.title: Public by default
principles.body: Use public repositories, public docs, and public grant updates whenever they can carry the claim.

principles.title: Integrate before rebuilding
principles.body: Frame the guild around practical coordination infrastructure and integrations with tools such as EAS, Gitcoin, Allo, KarmaGAP, and Green Goods.

principles.title: Chapter-informed tooling
principles.body: Show how tools support local chapters and real regenerative work, not software for its own sake.

projects.summary: Smart-contract-governed funding pools for DAOs and communities.
projects.description: Cookie Jar is listed in the Dev Guild public project surface as a funding primitive for communities: shared jars with allowlists, NFT gating, and configurable withdrawal rules.

seo.title: Greenpill Impact Framework · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Impact Framework connects public impact verification, Green Goods, and regenerative capital coordination.

projects.name: Greenpill Impact Framework
projects.summary: Public impact verification and capital-formation framework connected to Green Goods.
projects.description: The public companion thread frames the Greenpill Impact Framework as a way to connect Green Goods v1, impact, strategy, measurement, coordination, and capital formation. Internal framework details shaped the research artifact, but launch copy should point to the public Hub thread unless stewards approve more.

seo.title: Greenpill Podcast · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Greenpill Podcast is a long-running public media surface for regenerative crypto and public-goods coordination.

projects.name: Greenpill Podcast
projects.summary: Long-running public podcast about crypto-economic systems that create positive externalities.
projects.description: The Greenpill Podcast remains one of the network's strongest public media surfaces, with the current Libsyn RSS snapshot exposing 296 public episodes from 2022 through 2026 and sub-series including VDAO x Greenpill and Network Nations.

seo.title: GreenWill · Greenpill Network
seo.description: GreenWill is a Greenpill Dev Guild reputation and pay-it-forward tooling project.

projects.name: GreenWill
projects.summary: Public-good reputation and pay-it-forward activity tooling.
projects.description: GreenWill is described in the research source inventory as an open Farcaster mini-app for accumulating Greenpill and ReFi reputation from pay-it-forward activity such as Octant, Gitcoin, and sponsored rounds.

seo.title: Public Goods Staking Protocol · Greenpill Network
seo.description: The Public Goods Staking Protocol is a Dev Guild workstream for recurring public-goods funding through validator infrastructure and vault routing.

projects.name: Public Goods Staking Protocol
projects.summary: Dev Guild workstream using validator infrastructure and vault routing to create recurring public-goods funding.
projects.description: The Public Goods Staking Protocol is a Greenpill Dev Guild workstream sourced from the Dev Guild Paragraph series. Public sources describe a path where validator infrastructure, Dappnode, Obol distributed validator technology, Lido CSM, and Octant-aligned vault routing can help communities generate recurring funding for public goods. Treat Squad Staking as part of this workstream, not a separate project seed in this batch.

seo.title: Green Goods · Greenpill Network
seo.description: Green Goods is a Greenpill Dev Guild PWA for source-backed regenerative impact verification and public proof.

projects.name: Green Goods
projects.summary: Offline-first impact verification for regenerative work.
projects.description: Green Goods is a mobile-first PWA built by the Greenpill Dev Guild to document and verify ecological and social work. Public sources describe an offline-first flow where operators approve submissions and impact can be anchored with Ethereum Attestation Service on Arbitrum, with Green Goods positioned as core infrastructure for public proof.
